fields.name: dataflow_projects_jobs_list
fields.description: List the jobs of a project. To list the jobs of a project in a region, we recommend using `projects.locations.jobs.list` with a [regional endpoint] (https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/concepts/regional-endpoints). To list the all jobs across all regions, use `projects.jobs.aggregated`. Using `projects.jobs.list` is not recommended, because you can only get the list of jobs that are running in `us-central1`. `projects.locations.jobs.list` and `projects.jobs.list` support filtering the list of jobs by name. Filtering by name isn't supported by `projects.jobs.aggregated`.

args.name: projectId
args.description: The project which owns the jobs.

args.name: filter
args.description: The kind of filter to use.

args.name: view
args.description: Deprecated. ListJobs always returns summaries now. Use GetJob for other JobViews.

args.name: pageSize
args.description: If there are many jobs, limit response to at most this many. The actual number of jobs returned will be the lesser of max_responses and an unspecified server-defined limit.

args.name: pageToken
args.description: Set this to the 'next_page_token' field of a previous response to request additional results in a long list.

args.name: location
args.description: The [regional endpoint] (https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/concepts/regional-endpoints) that contains this job.

args.name: name
args.description: Optional. The job name.

types.name: Job
types.description: Defines a job to be run by the Cloud Dataflow service. Do not enter confidential information when you supply string values using the API.

fields.name: id
fields.description: The unique ID of this job. This field is set by the Dataflow service when the job is created, and is immutable for the life of the job.

fields.name: projectId
fields.description: The ID of the Google Cloud project that the job belongs to.

fields.name: name
fields.description: Optional. The user-specified Dataflow job name. Only one active job with a given name can exist in a project within one region at any given time. Jobs in different regions can have the same name. If a caller attempts to create a job with the same name as an active job that already exists, the attempt returns the existing job. The name must match the regular expression `[a-z]([-a-z0-9]{0,1022}[a-z0-9])?`

fields.name: steps
fields.description: Exactly one of step or steps_location should be specified. The top-level steps that constitute the entire job. Only retrieved with JOB_VIEW_ALL.

fields.name: stepsLocation
fields.description: The Cloud Storage location where the steps are stored.

fields.name: currentStateTime
fields.description: The timestamp associated with the current state.

fields.name: createTime
fields.description: The timestamp when the job was initially created. Immutable and set by the Cloud Dataflow service.

fields.name: replaceJobId
fields.description: If this job is an update of an existing job, this field is the job ID of the job it replaced. When sending a `CreateJobRequest`, you can update a job by specifying it here. The job named here is stopped, and its intermediate state is transferred to this job.

fields.name: transformNameMapping
fields.description: Optional. The map of transform name prefixes of the job to be replaced to the corresponding name prefixes of the new job.

fields.name: clientRequestId
fields.description: The client's unique identifier of the job, re-used across retried attempts. If this field is set, the service will ensure its uniqueness. The request to create a job will fail if the service has knowledge of a previously submitted job with the same client's ID and job name. The caller may use this field to ensure idempotence of job creation across retried attempts to create a job. By default, the field is empty and, in that case, the service ignores it.

fields.name: replacedByJobId
fields.description: If another job is an update of this job (and thus, this job is in `JOB_STATE_UPDATED`), this field contains the ID of that job.

fields.name: tempFiles
fields.description: A set of files the system should be aware of that are used for temporary storage. These temporary files will be removed on job completion. No duplicates are allowed. No file patterns are supported. The supported files are: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

fields.name: labels
fields.description: User-defined labels for this job. The labels map can contain no more than 64 entries. Entries of the labels map are UTF8 strings that comply with the following restrictions: * Keys must conform to regexp: \p{Ll}\p{Lo}{0,62} * Values must conform to regexp: [\p{Ll}\p{Lo}\p{N}_-]{0,63} * Both keys and values are additionally constrained to be <= 128 bytes in size.

fields.name: location
fields.description: Optional. The [regional endpoint] (https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/concepts/regional-endpoints) that contains this job.

fields.name: stageStates
fields.description: This field may be mutated by the Cloud Dataflow service; callers cannot mutate it.

fields.name: startTime
fields.description: The timestamp when the job was started (transitioned to JOB_STATE_PENDING). Flexible resource scheduling jobs are started with some delay after job creation, so start_time is unset before start and is updated when the job is started by the Cloud Dataflow service. For other jobs, start_time always equals to create_time and is immutable and set by the Cloud Dataflow service.

fields.name: createdFromSnapshotId
fields.description: If this is specified, the job's initial state is populated from the given snapshot.

fields.name: satisfiesPzs
fields.description: Reserved for future use. This field is set only in responses from the server; it is ignored if it is set in any requests.

fields.name: satisfiesPzi
fields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use. This field is set only in responses from the server; it is ignored if it is set in any requests.

fields.name: pausable
fields.description: Output only. Indicates whether the job can be paused.

types.name: mutation_dataflow_projects_jobs_create_type
types.description: Optional. The type of Dataflow job.

types.name: Environment
types.description: Describes the environment in which a Dataflow Job runs.

fields.name: tempStoragePrefix
fields.description: The prefix of the resources the system should use for temporary storage. The system will append the suffix "/temp-{JOBNAME} to this resource prefix, where {JOBNAME} is the value of the job_name field. The resulting bucket and object prefix is used as the prefix of the resources used to store temporary data needed during the job execution. NOTE: This will override the value in taskrunner_settings. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

fields.name: clusterManagerApiService
fields.description: The type of cluster manager API to use. If unknown or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default. This should be in the form of the API service name, e.g. "compute.googleapis.com".

fields.name: experiments
fields.description: The list of experiments to enable. This field should be used for SDK related experiments and not for service related experiments. The proper field for service related experiments is service_options.

fields.name: serviceOptions
fields.description: Optional. The list of service options to enable. This field should be used for service related experiments only. These experiments, when graduating to GA, should be replaced by dedicated fields or become default (i.e. always on).

fields.name: serviceKmsKeyName
fields.description: Optional. If set, contains the Cloud KMS key identifier used to encrypt data at rest, AKA a Customer Managed Encryption Key (CMEK). Format: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING/cryptoKeys/KEY

fields.name: workerPools
fields.description: The worker pools. At least one "harness" worker pool must be specified in order for the job to have workers.

fields.name: userAgent
fields.description: Optional. A description of the process that generated the request.

fields.name: version
fields.description: A structure describing which components and their versions of the service are required in order to run the job.

fields.name: dataset
fields.description: Optional. The dataset for the current project where various workflow related tables are stored. The supported resource type is: Google BigQuery: bigquery.googleapis.com/{dataset}

fields.name: sdkPipelineOptions
fields.description: The Cloud Dataflow SDK pipeline options specified by the user. These options are passed through the service and are used to recreate the SDK pipeline options on the worker in a language agnostic and platform independent way.

fields.name: internalExperiments
fields.description: Experimental settings.

fields.name: serviceAccountEmail
fields.description: Optional. Identity to run virtual machines as. Defaults to the default account.

fields.name: workerRegion
fields.description: Optional. The Compute Engine region (https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/regions-zones) in which worker processing should occur, e.g. "us-west1". Mutually exclusive with worker_zone. If neither worker_region nor worker_zone is specified, default to the control plane's region.

fields.name: workerZone
fields.description: Optional. The Compute Engine zone (https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/regions-zones) in which worker processing should occur, e.g. "us-west1-a". Mutually exclusive with worker_region. If neither worker_region nor worker_zone is specified, a zone in the control plane's region is chosen based on available capacity.

fields.name: useStreamingEngineResourceBasedBilling
fields.description: Output only. Whether the job uses the Streaming Engine resource-based billing model.

fields.name: usePublicIps
fields.description: Optional. True when any worker pool that uses public IPs is present.

types.name: WorkerPool
types.description: Describes one particular pool of Cloud Dataflow workers to be instantiated by the Cloud Dataflow service in order to perform the computations required by a job. Note that a workflow job may use multiple pools, in order to match the various computational requirements of the various stages of the job.

fields.name: kind
fields.description: The kind of the worker pool; currently only `harness` and `shuffle` are supported.

fields.name: numWorkers
fields.description: Number of Google Compute Engine workers in this pool needed to execute the job. If zero or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

fields.name: packages
fields.description: Packages to be installed on workers.

fields.name: machineType
fields.description: Machine type (e.g. "n1-standard-1"). If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

fields.name: diskSizeGb
fields.description: Size of root disk for VMs, in GB. If zero or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

fields.name: diskType
fields.description: Type of root disk for VMs. If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

fields.name: diskProvisionedIops
fields.description: Optional. IOPS provisioned for the root disk for VMs.

fields.name: diskProvisionedThroughputMibps
fields.description: Optional. Throughput provisioned for the root disk for VMs.

fields.name: diskSourceImage
fields.description: Fully qualified source image for disks.

fields.name: zone
fields.description: Zone to run the worker pools in. If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

fields.name: onHostMaintenance
fields.description: The action to take on host maintenance, as defined by the Google Compute Engine API.

fields.name: dataDisks
fields.description: Data disks that are used by a VM in this workflow.

fields.name: metadata
fields.description: Metadata to set on the Google Compute Engine VMs.

fields.name: poolArgs
fields.description: Extra arguments for this worker pool.

fields.name: network
fields.description: Network to which VMs will be assigned. If empty or unspecified, the service will use the network "default".

fields.name: subnetwork
fields.description: Subnetwork to which VMs will be assigned, if desired. Expected to be of the form "regions/REGION/subnetworks/SUBNETWORK".

fields.name: workerHarnessContainerImage
fields.description: Required. Docker container image that executes the Cloud Dataflow worker harness, residing in Google Container Registry. Deprecated for the Fn API path. Use sdk_harness_container_images instead.

fields.name: numThreadsPerWorker
fields.description: The number of threads per worker harness. If empty or unspecified, the service will choose a number of threads (according to the number of cores on the selected machine type for batch, or 1 by convention for streaming).

fields.name: sdkHarnessContainerImages
fields.description: Set of SDK harness containers needed to execute this pipeline. This will only be set in the Fn API path. For non-cross-language pipelines this should have only one entry. Cross-language pipelines will have two or more entries.

types.name: Package
types.description: The packages that must be installed in order for a worker to run the steps of the Cloud Dataflow job that will be assigned to its worker pool. This is the mechanism by which the Cloud Dataflow SDK causes code to be loaded onto the workers. For example, the Cloud Dataflow Java SDK might use this to install jars containing the user's code and all of the various dependencies (libraries, data files, etc.) required in order for that code to run.

fields.name: name

types.name: Step
types.description: Defines a particular step within a Cloud Dataflow job. A job consists of multiple steps, each of which performs some specific operation as part of the overall job. Data is typically passed from one step to another as part of the job. **Note:** The properties of this object are not stable and might change. Here's an example of a sequence of steps which together implement a Map-Reduce job: * Read a collection of data from some source, parsing the collection's elements. * Validate the elements. * Apply a user-defined function to map each element to some value and extract an element-specific key value. * Group elements with the same key into a single element with that key, transforming a multiply-keyed collection into a uniquely-keyed collection. * Write the elements out to some data sink. Note that the Cloud Dataflow service may be used to run many different types of jobs, not just Map-Reduce.

A job in the `JOB_STATE_RUNNING` state may asynchronously enter a terminal state. After a job has reached a terminal state, no further state updates may be made. This field might be mutated by the Dataflow service; callers cannot mutate it.

types.name: mutation_dataflow_projects_jobs_create_requestedState
types.description: The job's requested state. Applies to `UpdateJob` requests. Set `requested_state` with `UpdateJob` requests to switch between the states `JOB_STATE_STOPPED` and `JOB_STATE_RUNNING`. You can also use `UpdateJob` requests to change a job's state from `JOB_STATE_RUNNING` to `JOB_STATE_CANCELLED`, `JOB_STATE_DONE`, or `JOB_STATE_DRAINED`. These states irrevocably terminate the job if it hasn't already reached a terminal state. This field has no effect on `CreateJob` requests.

types.name: WorkerMessage_Input
types.description: WorkerMessage provides information to the backend about a worker.

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: Labels are used to group WorkerMessages. For example, a worker_message about a particular container might have the labels: { "JOB_ID": "2015-04-22", "WORKER_ID": "wordcount-vm-2015…" "CONTAINER_TYPE": "worker", "CONTAINER_ID": "ac1234def"} Label tags typically correspond to Label enum values. However, for ease of development other strings can be used as tags. LABEL_UNSPECIFIED should not be used here.

inputFields.name: time
inputFields.description: The timestamp of the worker_message.

types.name: WorkerHealthReport_Input
types.description: WorkerHealthReport contains information about the health of a worker. The VM should be identified by the labels attached to the WorkerMessage that this health ping belongs to.

inputFields.name: vmIsHealthy
inputFields.description: Whether the VM is currently healthy.

inputFields.name: vmIsBroken
inputFields.description: Whether the VM is in a permanently broken state. Broken VMs should be abandoned or deleted ASAP to avoid assigning or completing any work.

inputFields.name: vmBrokenCode
inputFields.description: Code to describe a specific reason, if known, that a VM has reported broken state.

inputFields.name: msg
inputFields.description: Message describing any unusual health reports.

inputFields.name: vmStartupTime
inputFields.description: The time the VM was booted.

inputFields.name: reportInterval
inputFields.description: The interval at which the worker is sending health reports. The default value of 0 should be interpreted as the field is not being explicitly set by the worker.

inputFields.name: pods
inputFields.description: The pods running on the worker. See: http://kubernetes.io/v1.1/docs/api-reference/v1/definitions.html#_v1_pod This field is used by the worker to send the status of the indvidual containers running on each worker.

types.name: WorkerMessageCode_Input
types.description: A message code is used to report status and error messages to the service. The message codes are intended to be machine readable. The service will take care of translating these into user understandable messages if necessary. Example use cases: 1. Worker processes reporting successful startup. 2. Worker processes reporting specific errors (e.g. package staging failure).

inputFields.name: code
inputFields.description: The code is a string intended for consumption by a machine that identifies the type of message being sent. Examples: 1. "HARNESS_STARTED" might be used to indicate the worker harness has started. 2. "GCS_DOWNLOAD_ERROR" might be used to indicate an error downloading a Cloud Storage file as part of the boot process of one of the worker containers. This is a string and not an enum to make it easy to add new codes without waiting for an API change.

inputFields.name: parameters
inputFields.description: Parameters contains specific information about the code. This is a struct to allow parameters of different types. Examples: 1. For a "HARNESS_STARTED" message parameters might provide the name of the worker and additional data like timing information. 2. For a "GCS_DOWNLOAD_ERROR" parameters might contain fields listing the Cloud Storage objects being downloaded and fields containing errors. In general complex data structures should be avoided. If a worker needs to send a specific and complicated data structure then please consider defining a new proto and adding it to the data oneof in WorkerMessageResponse. Conventions: Parameters should only be used for information that isn't typically passed as a label. hostname and other worker identifiers should almost always be passed as labels since they will be included on most messages.

types.name: ResourceUtilizationReport_Input
types.description: Worker metrics exported from workers. This contains resource utilization metrics accumulated from a variety of sources. For more information, see go/df-resource-signals.

inputFields.name: gpuUsage

inputFields.name: memoryInfo

inputFields.name: rate
inputFields.description: Average CPU utilization rate (% non-idle cpu / second) since previous sample.

types.name: WorkerShutdownNotice_Input

inputFields.name: reason
inputFields.description: The reason for the worker shutdown. Current possible values are: "UNKNOWN": shutdown reason is unknown. "PREEMPTION": shutdown reason is preemption. Other possible reasons may be added in the future.

types.name: WorkerLifecycleEvent_Input
types.description: A report of an event in a worker's lifecycle. The proto contains one event, because the worker is expected to asynchronously send each message immediately after the event. Due to this asynchrony, messages may arrive out of order (or missing), and it is up to the consumer to interpret. The timestamp of the event is in the enclosing WorkerMessage proto.

inputFields.name: metadata
inputFields.description: Other stats that can accompany an event. E.g. { "downloaded_bytes" : "123456" }

inputFields.name: containerStartTime
inputFields.description: The start time of this container. All events will report this so that events can be grouped together across container/VM restarts.

types.name: mutationInput_dataflow_projects_workerMessages_input_workerMessages_items_workerLifecycleEvent_event
types.description: The event being reported.

types.name: WorkerThreadScalingReport_Input

inputFields.name: pcollectionsSampledCount

inputFields.name: exceptionsSampledCount

inputFields.name: bytesWrittenDelta

inputFields.name: translationErrorsCount
inputFields.description: Optional. Delta of errors counts from retrieving, or translating the samples from previous report.

inputFields.name: persistenceErrorsCount
inputFields.description: Optional. Delta of errors counts from persisting the samples from previous report.

types.name: StreamingScalingReport_Input

inputFields.name: maximumBundleCount

inputFields.name: outstandingBundleCount

inputFields.name: outstandingBytes

inputFields.name: maximumBytes

types.name: PerWorkerMetrics_Input
types.description: Per worker metrics.

inputFields.name: perStepNamespaceMetrics
inputFields.description: Optional. Metrics for a particular unfused step and namespace.

types.name: PerStepNamespaceMetrics_Input
types.description: Metrics for a particular unfused step and namespace. A metric is uniquely identified by the `metrics_namespace`, `original_step`, `metric name` and `metric_labels`.

inputFields.name: metricsNamespace
inputFields.description: The namespace of these metrics on the worker.

inputFields.name: originalStep
inputFields.description: The original system name of the unfused step that these metrics are reported from.

inputFields.name: metricValues
inputFields.description: Optional. Metrics that are recorded for this namespace and unfused step.

types.name: MetricValue_Input
types.description: The value of a metric along with its name and labels.

inputFields.name: metric
inputFields.description: Base name for this metric.

inputFields.name: metricLabels
inputFields.description: Optional. Set of metric labels for this metric.

inputFields.name: valueInt64
inputFields.description: Integer value of this metric.

types.name: DataflowHistogramValue_Input
types.description: Summary statistics for a population of values. HistogramValue contains a sequence of buckets and gives a count of values that fall into each bucket. Bucket boundares are defined by a formula and bucket widths are either fixed or exponentially increasing.

inputFields.name: count
inputFields.description: Number of values recorded in this histogram.

inputFields.name: bucketCounts
inputFields.description: Optional. The number of values in each bucket of the histogram, as described in `bucket_options`. `bucket_counts` should contain N values, where N is the number of buckets specified in `bucket_options`. If `bucket_counts` has fewer than N values, the remaining values are assumed to be 0.

types.name: BucketOptions_Input
types.description: `BucketOptions` describes the bucket boundaries used in the histogram.

types.name: Linear_Input
types.description: Linear buckets with the following boundaries for indices in 0 to n-1. - i in [0, n-1]: [start + (i)*width, start + (i+1)*width)

inputFields.name: numberOfBuckets
inputFields.description: Must be greater than 0.

inputFields.name: width
inputFields.description: Distance between bucket boundaries. Must be greater than 0.

inputFields.name: start
inputFields.description: Lower bound of the first bucket.

types.name: Base2Exponent_Input
types.description: Exponential buckets where the growth factor between buckets is `2**(2**-scale)`. e.g. for `scale=1` growth factor is `2**(2**(-1))=sqrt(2)`. `n` buckets will have the following boundaries. - 0th: [0, gf) - i in [1, n-1]: [gf^(i), gf^(i+1))

inputFields.name: numberOfBuckets
inputFields.description: Must be greater than 0.

inputFields.name: scale
inputFields.description: Must be between -3 and 3. This forces the growth factor of the bucket boundaries to be between `2^(1/8)` and `256`.

types.name: OutlierStats_Input
types.description: Statistics for the underflow and overflow bucket.

inputFields.name: underflowCount
inputFields.description: Number of values that are smaller than the lower bound of the smallest bucket.

inputFields.name: underflowMean
inputFields.description: Mean of values in the undeflow bucket.

inputFields.name: overflowCount
inputFields.description: Number of values that are larger than the upper bound of the largest bucket.

inputFields.name: overflowMean
inputFields.description: Mean of values in the overflow bucket.

types.name: DataflowGaugeValue_Input

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: The value of the gauge.

inputFields.name: measuredTime
inputFields.description: The timestamp when the gauge was recorded.

types.name: Job_Input
types.description: Defines a job to be run by the Cloud Dataflow service. Do not enter confidential information when you supply string values using the API.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: The unique ID of this job. This field is set by the Dataflow service when the job is created, and is immutable for the life of the job.

inputFields.name: projectId
inputFields.description: The ID of the Google Cloud project that the job belongs to.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Optional. The user-specified Dataflow job name. Only one active job with a given name can exist in a project within one region at any given time. Jobs in different regions can have the same name. If a caller attempts to create a job with the same name as an active job that already exists, the attempt returns the existing job. The name must match the regular expression `[a-z]([-a-z0-9]{0,1022}[a-z0-9])?`

inputFields.name: steps
inputFields.description: Exactly one of step or steps_location should be specified. The top-level steps that constitute the entire job. Only retrieved with JOB_VIEW_ALL.

inputFields.name: stepsLocation
inputFields.description: The Cloud Storage location where the steps are stored.

inputFields.name: currentStateTime
inputFields.description: The timestamp associated with the current state.

inputFields.name: createTime
inputFields.description: The timestamp when the job was initially created. Immutable and set by the Cloud Dataflow service.

inputFields.name: replaceJobId
inputFields.description: If this job is an update of an existing job, this field is the job ID of the job it replaced. When sending a `CreateJobRequest`, you can update a job by specifying it here. The job named here is stopped, and its intermediate state is transferred to this job.

inputFields.name: transformNameMapping
inputFields.description: Optional. The map of transform name prefixes of the job to be replaced to the corresponding name prefixes of the new job.

inputFields.name: clientRequestId
inputFields.description: The client's unique identifier of the job, re-used across retried attempts. If this field is set, the service will ensure its uniqueness. The request to create a job will fail if the service has knowledge of a previously submitted job with the same client's ID and job name. The caller may use this field to ensure idempotence of job creation across retried attempts to create a job. By default, the field is empty and, in that case, the service ignores it.

inputFields.name: replacedByJobId
inputFields.description: If another job is an update of this job (and thus, this job is in `JOB_STATE_UPDATED`), this field contains the ID of that job.

inputFields.name: tempFiles
inputFields.description: A set of files the system should be aware of that are used for temporary storage. These temporary files will be removed on job completion. No duplicates are allowed. No file patterns are supported. The supported files are: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

inputFields.name: labels
inputFields.description: User-defined labels for this job. The labels map can contain no more than 64 entries. Entries of the labels map are UTF8 strings that comply with the following restrictions: * Keys must conform to regexp: \p{Ll}\p{Lo}{0,62} * Values must conform to regexp: [\p{Ll}\p{Lo}\p{N}_-]{0,63} * Both keys and values are additionally constrained to be <= 128 bytes in size.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: Optional. The [regional endpoint] (https://cloud.google.com/dataflow/docs/concepts/regional-endpoints) that contains this job.

inputFields.name: stageStates
inputFields.description: This field may be mutated by the Cloud Dataflow service; callers cannot mutate it.

inputFields.name: startTime
inputFields.description: The timestamp when the job was started (transitioned to JOB_STATE_PENDING). Flexible resource scheduling jobs are started with some delay after job creation, so start_time is unset before start and is updated when the job is started by the Cloud Dataflow service. For other jobs, start_time always equals to create_time and is immutable and set by the Cloud Dataflow service.

inputFields.name: createdFromSnapshotId
inputFields.description: If this is specified, the job's initial state is populated from the given snapshot.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzs
inputFields.description: Reserved for future use. This field is set only in responses from the server; it is ignored if it is set in any requests.

inputFields.name: satisfiesPzi
inputFields.description: Output only. Reserved for future use. This field is set only in responses from the server; it is ignored if it is set in any requests.

inputFields.name: pausable
inputFields.description: Output only. Indicates whether the job can be paused.

types.name: Environment_Input
types.description: Describes the environment in which a Dataflow Job runs.

inputFields.name: tempStoragePrefix
inputFields.description: The prefix of the resources the system should use for temporary storage. The system will append the suffix "/temp-{JOBNAME} to this resource prefix, where {JOBNAME} is the value of the job_name field. The resulting bucket and object prefix is used as the prefix of the resources used to store temporary data needed during the job execution. NOTE: This will override the value in taskrunner_settings. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

inputFields.name: clusterManagerApiService
inputFields.description: The type of cluster manager API to use. If unknown or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default. This should be in the form of the API service name, e.g. "compute.googleapis.com".

inputFields.name: experiments
inputFields.description: The list of experiments to enable. This field should be used for SDK related experiments and not for service related experiments. The proper field for service related experiments is service_options.

inputFields.name: serviceOptions
inputFields.description: Optional. The list of service options to enable. This field should be used for service related experiments only. These experiments, when graduating to GA, should be replaced by dedicated fields or become default (i.e. always on).

inputFields.name: serviceKmsKeyName
inputFields.description: Optional. If set, contains the Cloud KMS key identifier used to encrypt data at rest, AKA a Customer Managed Encryption Key (CMEK). Format: projects/PROJECT_ID/locations/LOCATION/keyRings/KEY_RING/cryptoKeys/KEY

inputFields.name: workerPools
inputFields.description: The worker pools. At least one "harness" worker pool must be specified in order for the job to have workers.

inputFields.name: userAgent
inputFields.description: Optional. A description of the process that generated the request.

inputFields.name: version
inputFields.description: A structure describing which components and their versions of the service are required in order to run the job.

inputFields.name: dataset
inputFields.description: Optional. The dataset for the current project where various workflow related tables are stored. The supported resource type is: Google BigQuery: bigquery.googleapis.com/{dataset}

inputFields.name: sdkPipelineOptions
inputFields.description: The Cloud Dataflow SDK pipeline options specified by the user. These options are passed through the service and are used to recreate the SDK pipeline options on the worker in a language agnostic and platform independent way.

inputFields.name: internalExperiments
inputFields.description: Experimental settings.

inputFields.name: serviceAccountEmail
inputFields.description: Optional. Identity to run virtual machines as. Defaults to the default account.

inputFields.name: workerRegion
inputFields.description: Optional. The Compute Engine region (https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/regions-zones) in which worker processing should occur, e.g. "us-west1". Mutually exclusive with worker_zone. If neither worker_region nor worker_zone is specified, default to the control plane's region.

inputFields.name: workerZone
inputFields.description: Optional. The Compute Engine zone (https://cloud.google.com/compute/docs/regions-zones/regions-zones) in which worker processing should occur, e.g. "us-west1-a". Mutually exclusive with worker_region. If neither worker_region nor worker_zone is specified, a zone in the control plane's region is chosen based on available capacity.

inputFields.name: useStreamingEngineResourceBasedBilling
inputFields.description: Output only. Whether the job uses the Streaming Engine resource-based billing model.

inputFields.name: usePublicIps
inputFields.description: Optional. True when any worker pool that uses public IPs is present.

types.name: WorkerPool_Input
types.description: Describes one particular pool of Cloud Dataflow workers to be instantiated by the Cloud Dataflow service in order to perform the computations required by a job. Note that a workflow job may use multiple pools, in order to match the various computational requirements of the various stages of the job.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of the worker pool; currently only `harness` and `shuffle` are supported.

inputFields.name: numWorkers
inputFields.description: Number of Google Compute Engine workers in this pool needed to execute the job. If zero or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: packages
inputFields.description: Packages to be installed on workers.

inputFields.name: machineType
inputFields.description: Machine type (e.g. "n1-standard-1"). If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: diskSizeGb
inputFields.description: Size of root disk for VMs, in GB. If zero or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: diskType
inputFields.description: Type of root disk for VMs. If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: diskProvisionedIops
inputFields.description: Optional. IOPS provisioned for the root disk for VMs.

inputFields.name: diskProvisionedThroughputMibps
inputFields.description: Optional. Throughput provisioned for the root disk for VMs.

inputFields.name: diskSourceImage
inputFields.description: Fully qualified source image for disks.

inputFields.name: zone
inputFields.description: Zone to run the worker pools in. If empty or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: onHostMaintenance
inputFields.description: The action to take on host maintenance, as defined by the Google Compute Engine API.

inputFields.name: dataDisks
inputFields.description: Data disks that are used by a VM in this workflow.

inputFields.name: metadata
inputFields.description: Metadata to set on the Google Compute Engine VMs.

inputFields.name: poolArgs
inputFields.description: Extra arguments for this worker pool.

inputFields.name: network
inputFields.description: Network to which VMs will be assigned. If empty or unspecified, the service will use the network "default".

inputFields.name: subnetwork
inputFields.description: Subnetwork to which VMs will be assigned, if desired. Expected to be of the form "regions/REGION/subnetworks/SUBNETWORK".

inputFields.name: workerHarnessContainerImage
inputFields.description: Required. Docker container image that executes the Cloud Dataflow worker harness, residing in Google Container Registry. Deprecated for the Fn API path. Use sdk_harness_container_images instead.

inputFields.name: numThreadsPerWorker
inputFields.description: The number of threads per worker harness. If empty or unspecified, the service will choose a number of threads (according to the number of cores on the selected machine type for batch, or 1 by convention for streaming).

inputFields.name: sdkHarnessContainerImages
inputFields.description: Set of SDK harness containers needed to execute this pipeline. This will only be set in the Fn API path. For non-cross-language pipelines this should have only one entry. Cross-language pipelines will have two or more entries.

types.name: Package_Input
types.description: The packages that must be installed in order for a worker to run the steps of the Cloud Dataflow job that will be assigned to its worker pool. This is the mechanism by which the Cloud Dataflow SDK causes code to be loaded onto the workers. For example, the Cloud Dataflow Java SDK might use this to install jars containing the user's code and all of the various dependencies (libraries, data files, etc.) required in order for that code to run.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name of the package.

inputFields.name: location
inputFields.description: The resource to read the package from. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/

types.name: TaskRunnerSettings_Input
types.description: Taskrunner configuration settings.

inputFields.name: taskUser
inputFields.description: The UNIX user ID on the worker VM to use for tasks launched by taskrunner; e.g. "root".

inputFields.name: taskGroup
inputFields.description: The UNIX group ID on the worker VM to use for tasks launched by taskrunner; e.g. "wheel".

inputFields.name: oauthScopes
inputFields.description: The OAuth2 scopes to be requested by the taskrunner in order to access the Cloud Dataflow API.

inputFields.name: baseUrl
inputFields.description: The base URL for the taskrunner to use when accessing Google Cloud APIs. When workers access Google Cloud APIs, they logically do so via relative URLs. If this field is specified, it supplies the base URL to use for resolving these relative URLs. The normative algorithm used is defined by RFC 1808, "Relative Uniform Resource Locators". If not specified, the default value is "http://www.googleapis.com/"

inputFields.name: dataflowApiVersion
inputFields.description: The API version of endpoint, e.g. "v1b3"

inputFields.name: baseTaskDir

inputFields.name: continueOnException

inputFields.name: logToSerialconsole
inputFields.description: Whether to send taskrunner log info to Google Compute Engine VM serial console.

inputFields.name: alsologtostderr
inputFields.description: Whether to also send taskrunner log info to stderr.

inputFields.name: logUploadLocation
inputFields.description: Indicates where to put logs. If this is not specified, the logs will not be uploaded. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

inputFields.name: logDir
inputFields.description: The directory on the VM to store logs.

inputFields.name: tempStoragePrefix
inputFields.description: The prefix of the resources the taskrunner should use for temporary storage. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

inputFields.name: harnessCommand
inputFields.description: The command to launch the worker harness.

inputFields.name: workflowFileName
inputFields.description: The file to store the workflow in.

inputFields.name: commandlinesFileName
inputFields.description: The file to store preprocessing commands in.

inputFields.name: vmId
inputFields.description: The ID string of the VM.

inputFields.name: languageHint
inputFields.description: The suggested backend language.

inputFields.name: streamingWorkerMainClass
inputFields.description: The streaming worker main class name.

types.name: WorkerSettings_Input

inputFields.name: baseUrl
inputFields.description: The base URL for accessing Google Cloud APIs. When workers access Google Cloud APIs, they logically do so via relative URLs. If this field is specified, it supplies the base URL to use for resolving these relative URLs. The normative algorithm used is defined by RFC 1808, "Relative Uniform Resource Locators". If not specified, the default value is "http://www.googleapis.com/"

inputFields.name: reportingEnabled
inputFields.description: Whether to send work progress updates to the service.

inputFields.name: servicePath
inputFields.description: The Cloud Dataflow service path relative to the root URL, for example, "dataflow/v1b3/projects".

inputFields.name: shuffleServicePath
inputFields.description: The Shuffle service path relative to the root URL, for example, "shuffle/v1beta1".

inputFields.name: workerId

inputFields.name: tempStoragePrefix
inputFields.description: The prefix of the resources the system should use for temporary storage. The supported resource type is: Google Cloud Storage: storage.googleapis.com/{bucket}/{object} bucket.storage.googleapis.com/{object}

types.name: Disk_Input
types.description: Describes the data disk used by a workflow job.

inputFields.name: sizeGb
inputFields.description: Size of disk in GB. If zero or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default.

inputFields.name: diskType
inputFields.description: Disk storage type, as defined by Google Compute Engine. This must be a disk type appropriate to the project and zone in which the workers will run. If unknown or unspecified, the service will attempt to choose a reasonable default. For example, the standard persistent disk type is a resource name typically ending in "pd-standard". If SSD persistent disks are available, the resource name typically ends with "pd-ssd". The actual valid values are defined the Google Compute Engine API, not by the Cloud Dataflow API; consult the Google Compute Engine documentation for more information about determining the set of available disk types for a particular project and zone. Google Compute Engine Disk types are local to a particular project in a particular zone, and so the resource name will typically look something like this: compute.googleapis.com/projects/project-id/zones/zone/diskTypes/pd-standard

inputFields.name: mountPoint
inputFields.description: Directory in a VM where disk is mounted.

types.name: AutoscalingSettings_Input
types.description: Settings for WorkerPool autoscaling.

inputFields.name: maxNumWorkers
inputFields.description: The maximum number of workers to cap scaling at.

types.name: SdkHarnessContainerImage_Input
types.description: Defines an SDK harness container for executing Dataflow pipelines.

inputFields.name: containerImage
inputFields.description: A docker container image that resides in Google Container Registry.

inputFields.name: useSingleCorePerContainer
inputFields.description: If true, recommends the Dataflow service to use only one core per SDK container instance with this image. If false (or unset) recommends using more than one core per SDK container instance with this image for efficiency. Note that Dataflow service may choose to override this property if needed.

inputFields.name: environmentId
inputFields.description: Environment ID for the Beam runner API proto Environment that corresponds to the current SDK Harness.

inputFields.name: capabilities
inputFields.description: The set of capabilities enumerated in the above Environment proto. See also [beam_runner_api.proto](https://github.com/apache/beam/blob/master/model/pipeline/src/main/proto/org/apache/beam/model/pipeline/v1/beam_runner_api.proto)

types.name: DebugOptions_Input
types.description: Describes any options that have an effect on the debugging of pipelines.

inputFields.name: enableHotKeyLogging
inputFields.description: Optional. When true, enables the logging of the literal hot key to the user's Cloud Logging.

types.name: DataSamplingConfig_Input

inputFields.name: behaviors
inputFields.description: List of given sampling behaviors to enable. For example, specifying behaviors = [ALWAYS_ON] samples in-flight elements but does not sample exceptions. Can be used to specify multiple behaviors like, behaviors = [ALWAYS_ON, EXCEPTIONS] for specifying periodic sampling and exception sampling. If DISABLED is in the list, then sampling will be disabled and ignore the other given behaviors. Ordering does not matter.

types.name: Step_Input
types.description: Defines a particular step within a Cloud Dataflow job. A job consists of multiple steps, each of which performs some specific operation as part of the overall job. Data is typically passed from one step to another as part of the job. **Note:** The properties of this object are not stable and might change. Here's an example of a sequence of steps which together implement a Map-Reduce job: * Read a collection of data from some source, parsing the collection's elements. * Validate the elements. * Apply a user-defined function to map each element to some value and extract an element-specific key value. * Group elements with the same key into a single element with that key, transforming a multiply-keyed collection into a uniquely-keyed collection. * Write the elements out to some data sink. Note that the Cloud Dataflow service may be used to run many different types of jobs, not just Map-Reduce.

inputFields.name: kind
inputFields.description: The kind of step in the Cloud Dataflow job.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: The name that identifies the step. This must be unique for each step with respect to all other steps in the Cloud Dataflow job.

inputFields.name: properties
inputFields.description: Named properties associated with the step. Each kind of predefined step has its own required set of properties. Must be provided on Create. Only retrieved with JOB_VIEW_ALL.

types.name: JobExecutionInfo_Input

types.name: PipelineDescription_Input

inputFields.name: originalPipelineTransform

inputFields.name: executionPipelineStage

inputFields.name: displayData

inputFields.name: stepNamesHash

types.name: TransformSummary_Input

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: SDK generated id of this transform instance.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: User provided name for this transform instance.

inputFields.name: displayData
inputFields.description: Transform-specific display data.

inputFields.name: outputCollectionName
inputFields.description: User names for all collection outputs to this transform.

inputFields.name: inputCollectionName
inputFields.description: User names for all collection inputs to this transform.

types.name: DisplayData_Input
types.description: Data provided with a pipeline or transform to provide descriptive info.

inputFields.name: key
inputFields.description: The key identifying the display data. This is intended to be used as a label for the display data when viewed in a dax monitoring system.

inputFields.name: namespace
inputFields.description: The namespace for the key. This is usually a class name or programming language namespace (i.e. python module) which defines the display data. This allows a dax monitoring system to specially handle the data and perform custom rendering.

inputFields.name: strValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of string type.

inputFields.name: int64Value
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of int64 type.

inputFields.name: floatValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of float type.

inputFields.name: javaClassValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of java class type.

inputFields.name: timestampValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of timestamp type.

inputFields.name: durationValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of duration type.

inputFields.name: boolValue
inputFields.description: Contains value if the data is of a boolean type.

inputFields.name: shortStrValue
inputFields.description: A possible additional shorter value to display. For example a java_class_name_value of com.mypackage.MyDoFn will be stored with MyDoFn as the short_str_value and com.mypackage.MyDoFn as the java_class_name value. short_str_value can be displayed and java_class_name_value will be displayed as a tooltip.

inputFields.name: url
inputFields.description: An optional full URL.

inputFields.name: label
inputFields.description: An optional label to display in a dax UI for the element.

types.name: ExecutionStageSummary_Input
types.description: Description of the composing transforms, names/ids, and input/outputs of a stage of execution. Some composing transforms and sources may have been generated by the Dataflow service during execution planning.

inputFields.name: name
inputFields.description: Dataflow service generated name for this stage.

inputFields.name: id
inputFields.description: Dataflow service generated id for this stage.

inputFields.name: workItemId

inputFields.description: The report index. When a WorkItem is leased, the lease will contain an initial report index. When a WorkItem's status is reported to the system, the report should be sent with that report index, and the response will contain the index the worker should use for the next report. Reports received with unexpected index values will be rejected by the service. In order to preserve idempotency, the worker should not alter the contents of a report, even if the worker must submit the same report multiple times before getting back a response. The worker should not submit a subsequent report until the response for the previous report had been received from the service.

You can find out more about this error model and how to work with it in the [API Design Guide](https://cloud.google.com/apis/design/errors).

types.name: Histogram_Input
types.description: Histogram of value counts for a distribution. Buckets have an inclusive lower bound and exclusive upper bound and use "1,2,5 bucketing": The first bucket range is from [0,1) and all subsequent bucket boundaries are powers of ten multiplied by 1, 2, or 5. Thus, bucket boundaries are 0, 1, 2, 5, 10, 20, 50, 100, 200, 500, 1000, ... Negative values are not supported.

inputFields.name: bucketCounts
inputFields.description: Counts of values in each bucket. For efficiency, prefix and trailing buckets with count = 0 are elided. Buckets can store the full range of values of an unsigned long, with ULLONG_MAX falling into the 59th bucket with range [1e19, 2e19).

inputFields.name: firstBucketOffset
inputFields.description: Starting index of first stored bucket. The non-inclusive upper-bound of the ith bucket is given by: pow(10,(i-first_bucket_offset)/3) * (1,2,5)[(i-first_bucket_offset)%3]

inputFields.description: Metric aggregation kind. The possible metric aggregation kinds are "Sum", "Max", "Min", "Mean", "Set", "And", "Or", and "Distribution". The specified aggregation kind is case-insensitive. If omitted, this is not an aggregated value but instead a single metric sample value.

inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for aggregation kinds "Sum", "Max", "Min", "And", and "Or". The possible value types are Long, Double, and Boolean.

inputFields.name: meanCount
inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for the "Mean" aggregation kind. This holds the count of the aggregated values and is used in combination with mean_sum above to obtain the actual mean aggregate value. The only possible value type is Long.

inputFields.name: set
inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for the "Set" aggregation kind. The only possible value type is a list of Values whose type can be Long, Double, String, or BoundedTrie according to the metric's type. All Values in the list must be of the same type.

inputFields.name: trie
inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for the "Trie" aggregation kind. The only possible value type is a BoundedTrieNode.

inputFields.name: boundedTrie
inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for the "Trie" aggregation kind. The only possible value type is a BoundedTrieNode. Introduced this field to avoid breaking older SDKs when Dataflow service starts to populate the `bounded_trie` field.

inputFields.name: distribution

inputFields.name: gauge
inputFields.description: A struct value describing properties of a Gauge. Metrics of gauge type show the value of a metric across time, and is aggregated based on the newest value.

inputFields.name: internal
inputFields.description: Worker-computed aggregate value for internal use by the Dataflow service.

inputFields.name: updateTime
inputFields.description: Timestamp associated with the metric value. Optional when workers are reporting work progress; it will be filled in responses from the metrics API.

types.description: Represents the level of parallelism in a WorkItem's input, reported by the worker.

inputFields.name: isInfinite
inputFields.description: Specifies whether the parallelism is infinite. If true, "value" is ignored. Infinite parallelism means the service will assume that the work item can always be split into more non-empty work items by dynamic splitting. This is a work-around for lack of support for infinity by the current JSON-based Java RPC stack.

inputFields.name: value
inputFields.description: Specifies the level of parallelism in case it is finite.

types.name: DynamicSourceSplit_Input
types.description: When a task splits using WorkItemStatus.dynamic_source_split, this message describes the two parts of the split relative to the description of the current task's input.

types.name: DerivedSource_Input
types.description: Specification of one of the bundles produced as a result of splitting a Source (e.g. when executing a SourceSplitRequest, or when splitting an active task using WorkItemStatus.dynamic_source_split), relative to the source being split.

types.name: Source_Input
types.description: A source that records can be read and decoded from.

inputFields.name: spec
inputFields.description: The source to read from, plus its parameters.

inputFields.name: codec
inputFields.description: The codec to use to decode data read from the source.

inputFields.name: baseSpecs
inputFields.description: While splitting, sources may specify the produced bundles as differences against another source, in order to save backend-side memory and allow bigger jobs. For details, see SourceSplitRequest. To support this use case, the full set of parameters of the source is logically obtained by taking the latest explicitly specified value of each parameter in the order: base_specs (later items win), spec (overrides anything in base_specs).

inputFields.name: doesNotNeedSplitting
inputFields.description: Setting this value to true hints to the framework that the source doesn't need splitting, and using SourceSplitRequest on it would yield SOURCE_SPLIT_OUTCOME_USE_CURRENT. E.g. a file splitter may set this to true when splitting a single file into a set of byte ranges of appropriate size, and set this to false when splitting a filepattern into individual files. However, for efficiency, a file splitter may decide to produce file subranges directly from the filepattern to avoid a splitting round-trip. See SourceSplitRequest for an overview of the splitting process. This field is meaningful only in the Source objects populated by the user (e.g. when filling in a DerivedSource). types.name: SourceSplitRequest
types.description: Represents the operation to split a high-level Source specification into bundles (parts for parallel processing). At a high level, splitting of a source into bundles happens as follows: SourceSplitRequest is applied to the source. If it returns SOURCE_SPLIT_OUTCOME_USE_CURRENT, no further splitting happens and the source is used "as is". Otherwise, splitting is applied recursively to each produced DerivedSource. As an optimization, for any Source, if its does_not_need_splitting is true, the framework assumes that splitting this source would return SOURCE_SPLIT_OUTCOME_USE_CURRENT, and doesn't initiate a SourceSplitRequest. This applies both to the initial source being split and to bundles produced from it.

types.name: String

types.name: __Schema
types.description: A GraphQL Schema defines the capabilities of a GraphQL server. It exposes all available types and directives on the server, as well as the entry points for query, mutation, and subscription operations.

fields.name: types
fields.description: A list of all types supported by this server.

fields.name: queryType
fields.description: The type that query operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: mutationType
fields.description: If this server supports mutation, the type that mutation operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: subscriptionType
fields.description: If this server support subscription, the type that subscription operations will be rooted at.

fields.name: directives
fields.description: A list of all directives supported by this server.

types.name: __Type
types.description:
The fundamental unit of any GraphQL Schema is the type. There are many kinds of types in GraphQL as represented by the `__TypeKind` enum.

Depending on the kind of a type, certain fields describe information about that type. Scalar types provide no information beyond a name, description and optional `specifiedByURL`, while Enum types provide their values. Object and Interface types provide the fields they describe. Abstract types, Union and Interface, provide the Object types possible at runtime. List and NonNull types compose other types.

types.name: __TypeKind
types.description: An enum describing what kind of type a given `__Type` is.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a scalar.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an object. `fields` and `interfaces` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an interface. `fields`, `interfaces`, and `possibleTypes` are valid fields.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a union. `possibleTypes` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an enum. `enumValues` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is an input object. `inputFields` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: LIST
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a list. `ofType` is a valid field.

enumValues.name: NON_NULL
enumValues.description: Indicates this type is a non-null. `ofType` is a valid field.

types.name: __Field
types.description: Object and Interface types are described by a list of Fields, each of which has a name, potentially a list of arguments, and a return type.

types.name: __InputValue
types.description: Arguments provided to Fields or Directives and the input fields of an InputObject are represented as Input Values which describe their type and optionally a default value.

fields.name: defaultValue
fields.description: A GraphQL-formatted string representing the default value for this input value.

types.name: __EnumValue
types.description: One possible value for a given Enum. Enum values are unique values, not a placeholder for a string or numeric value. However an Enum value is returned in a JSON response as a string.

types.name: __Directive
types.description:
A Directive provides a way to describe alternate runtime execution and type validation behavior in a GraphQL document.

In some cases, you need to provide options to alter GraphQL's execution behavior in ways field arguments will not suffice, such as conditionally including or skipping a field. Directives provide this by describing additional information to the executor.

types.name: __DirectiveLocation
types.description: A Directive can be adjacent to many parts of the GraphQL language, a __DirectiveLocation describes one such possible adjacencies.

enumValues.name: QUERY
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a query operation.

enumValues.name: MUTATION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a mutation operation.

enumValues.name: SUBSCRIPTION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a subscription operation.

enumValues.name: FIELD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment definition.

enumValues.name: FRAGMENT_SPREAD
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a fragment spread.

enumValues.name: INLINE_FRAGMENT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an inline fragment.

enumValues.name: VARIABLE_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a variable definition.

enumValues.name: SCHEMA
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a schema definition.

enumValues.name: SCALAR
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a scalar definition.

enumValues.name: OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an object type definition.

enumValues.name: FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a field definition.

enumValues.name: ARGUMENT_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an argument definition.

enumValues.name: INTERFACE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an interface definition.

enumValues.name: UNION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to a union definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum definition.

enumValues.name: ENUM_VALUE
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an enum value definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_OBJECT
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object type definition.

enumValues.name: INPUT_FIELD_DEFINITION
enumValues.description: Location adjacent to an input object field definition.

directives.name: skip
directives.description: Directs the executor to skip this field or fragment when the `if` argument is true.

args.name: if
args.description: Skipped when true.

directives.name: include
directives.description: Directs the executor to include this field or fragment only when the `if` argument is true.
